jokes.text: That sign says 'Applesauce.' No, no, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'
jokes.setup: Hank points to an applause sign and misreads it as 'applesauce'
jokes.punchline: The immediate correction reveals it was deliberate misdirection
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection with good economy, typical Hank warmup material

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: That sign says 'Applesauce.' No, no, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'

jokes.text: Julia Roberts collapsed on set, but it turns out it had just finally dawned on her that she had married Lyle Lovett, which would cause anyone just to pass out
jokes.setup: Celebrity news about Julia Roberts collapsing with speculation about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: The real reason was realizing she married an unconventional-looking musician
jokes.explanation: Sharp celebrity roast with good structure and timing, references real odd-couple marriage

dialogue.line: Julia Roberts collapsed on set, but it turns out it had just finally dawned on her that she had married Lyle Lovett, which would cause anyone just to pass out

dialogue.line: The defense department canceled the Star Wars program, which is a shame because it was the most consistent work Mark Hamill ever had

jokes.text: The prop master is flipping me off. That's for machine-gun fire.
jokes.setup: Larry trying out different alarm clock sounds

jokes.explanation: Good behind-the-scenes moment showing Larry's paranoid interpretation

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: The prop master is flipping me off. That's for machine-gun fire.

jokes.text: Where'd you learn to do that, jake? / Juilliard. / Money well spent, jake.

jokes.explanation: Classic put-down with good setup about overqualification

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: Juilliard.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Money well spent, jake.

jokes.text: Can you imagine waking up to that? Actually, I was wondering if the alarm clock was free later tonight.

jokes.punchline: Larry expresses interest in the sexual-sounding alarm
jokes.explanation: Reveals Larry's loneliness/desperation through sexual innuendo

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Can you imagine waking up to that? Actually, I was wondering if the alarm clock was free later tonight.

jokes.text: Oh, fucking moron-- Leaving us to go on tour with Amy Grant. Jesus! / Artie, he found god. / Blah, blah, blah.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Colin leaving the show for Amy Grant tour
jokes.punchline: Artie's dismissive 'blah, blah, blah' response to spiritual awakening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie character moment - cynical, profane dismissal of religious experience

dialogue.line: Oh, fucking moron-- Leaving us to go on tour with Amy Grant. Jesus!

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: he found god.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Blah, blah, blah.

jokes.text: Is anyone buying that accent? I mean, it's Yakov Smirnoff time.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jake Woodward's performance
jokes.punchline: Comparing Jake's fake accent to the notoriously fake-sounding comedian
jokes.explanation: Good reference joke showing Hank's awareness of show business phoniness

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Is anyone buying that accent? I mean, it's Yakov Smirnoff time.

jokes.text: What are you grabbing my elbow? / Am I? / Yeah. You grabbed my elbow. Please. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Tense conversation about hiring decisions
jokes.punchline: Awkward physical contact and overly polite correction
jokes.explanation: Subtle cringe moment showing Hank's discomfort with confrontation

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What are you grabbing my elbow?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Am I?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yeah. You grabbed my elbow. Please. Thank you.

jokes.text: You're fired. Ha ha ha! / All right. You see, now, that's funny. Let's take a second and examine, why is that funny, exactly?
jokes.setup: Artie jokingly fires Hank
jokes.punchline: Hank's academic analysis of why the joke worked
jokes.explanation: Meta-comedy about comedy itself, very much in Larry Sanders Show style

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You're fired. Ha ha ha!

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: All right. You see, now, that's funny. Let's take a second and examine, why is that funny, exactly?

jokes.text: Oh, fuck me. Poker at James Coburn's place! Oh, god. I forgot.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes he's forgotten something important
jokes.punchline: The dramatic realization of missing celebrity poker game
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry torn between relationship and celebrity social obligations

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Oh, fuck me. Poker at James Coburn's place! Oh, god. I forgot.

jokes.text: I agree with Artie, except about screwing Hank. That's a personal judgment call.
jokes.setup: Larry says 'screw Hank' in the dismissive sense
jokes.punchline: Jake interprets it sexually and declares neutrality
jokes.explanation: Basic double entendre but delivered with good timing

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: I agree with Artie, except about screwing Hank. That's a personal judgment call.

jokes.text: Mom and dad couldn't believe it the first time I wore pants. Give me a break.
jokes.setup: Dora mentions parents' surprise about Francine dating Larry again
jokes.punchline: Francine deflects by saying they're surprised by everything
jokes.explanation: Good character defense mechanism, shows Francine's wit and independence

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: Mom and dad couldn't believe it the first time I wore pants. Give me a break.

jokes.text: Is that Aaron Spelling's house? / No, that's the Mormon Tabernacle.

jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response comparing it to religious architecture

dialogue.speaker: Dora
dialogue.line: Is that Aaron Spelling's house?

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: No, that's the Mormon Tabernacle.

jokes.text: Don't forget to flush. [Chuckles] Or not. It's up to you.
jokes.setup: Larry gives bathroom directions to Dora
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-explanation about flushing options
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry awkwardness - trying too hard to be accommodating

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Don't forget to flush. Or not. It's up to you.

jokes.text: I can't have dinner, I'm filled up with pudding.
jokes.setup: Francine suggests they can still have dinner
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous excuse about being full from pudding
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Larry excuse - childish and avoidant, highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I can't have dinner, I'm filled up with pudding.

jokes.text: You're out of toilet paper. / You know what it is? In most of the relationships you've been involved in, you've been Larry Sanders-- the Larry Sanders.
jokes.setup: Dora interrupts with practical concern during relationship discussion
jokes.punchline: Artie continues his analysis while ignoring the toilet paper comment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - mundane interruption of pseudo-profound relationship advice

dialogue.speaker: Dora
dialogue.line: You're out of toilet paper.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You know what it is? In most of the relationships you've been involved in, you've been Larry Sanders-- the Larry Sanders.

jokes.text: God, you should have a column.
jokes.setup: Artie gives relationship advice while handling business papers
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic appreciation of Artie's multitasking wisdom
jokes.explanation: Nice sarcastic beat about Artie's ability to give advice while doing business

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: God, you should have a column.

jokes.text: Give me an 'L,' give me an 'A,' give me an 'R,' give me an 'R,' give me a 'Y.' What's that spell? Come on! What's that spell? / Larry. / God damn right, Larry.

dialogue.speaker: Hank

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: God damn right, Larry.

jokes.text: Or to use your musical lingo, a couple of half notes. / [Both chuckle] / It's not a problem. I've got an easy schedule. / You see, Larry... 'Schedule.'

jokes.punchline: Hank mocking Jake's pronunciation of 'schedule' in British accent

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: No, but I think I sold him some pot at Oxford. / He was there. Let me tell you. He definitely inhaled. He just hasn't exhaled yet.

jokes.text: Step on my turf again, and you're going down. You dig? / Chill out. / Yeah, well, I'll chill out when you fucking get out.

jokes.explanation: Good character conflict showing generational and cultural clash

dialogue.line: Step on my turf again, and you're going down. You dig?

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: Chill out.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'll chill out when you fucking get out.

jokes.text: [British accent] Oh, am I? / Yes, you are. / Am I-- am I really? / [American accent] I'm warning you.
jokes.setup: Physical confrontation between Hank and Jake
jokes.punchline: Hank switching between accents mid-fight
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd moment - Hank's accent confusion during confrontation shows his pretense

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, am I?

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: Yes, you are.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Am I-- am I really?

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm warning you.

jokes.text: A double dollop of icing.
jokes.setup: Artie compliments Francine, then meets her sister
jokes.punchline: Extending the dessert metaphor to include both women
jokes.explanation: Typical Artie charm - extending his compliment metaphor smoothly

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: A double dollop of icing.

jokes.text: God, tonight's show was the cake, but you definitely are the icing.
jokes.setup: Artie greeting Francine
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top compliment using cake metaphor
jokes.explanation: Pure Artie schmooze - dated but earnest charm offensive that's slightly creepy.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: God, tonight's show was the cake, but you definitely are the icing.

jokes.text: Good. That would be great. / Together like this? Hey, great. Wouldn't that be great? Screw it.
jokes.setup: Francine sarcastically suggests they both quit their jobs
jokes.punchline: Larry's oblivious enthusiasm for the obviously sarcastic suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obliviousness - missing obvious sarcasm and doubling down

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Good. That would be great. / Together like this? Hey, great. Wouldn't that be great? Screw it.

jokes.text: Did any of Johnny's wives work? / Well, they were fully functional. Is that the question?
jokes.setup: Larry asks if Johnny Carson's wives had careers
jokes.punchline: Artie interprets 'work' as mechanical functionality
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect Artie delivery - treating wives like machinery

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Did any of Johnny's wives work?

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, they were fully functional. Is that the question?

jokes.text: All of Johnny's wives had the same first name, so that confused things from the get-go.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Johnny Carson's multiple marriages
jokes.punchline: Claiming all wives had same name as explanation for confusion
jokes.explanation: Absurd show business logic from Artie - treating practical confusion as romantic

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: All of Johnny's wives had the same first name, so that confused things from the get-go.

jokes.text: I fired her ass. She was my secretary.
jokes.setup: Larry asks how Artie handled his working wife
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of firing his secretary wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Artie moment - casual cruelty delivered with perfect timing and economy

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I fired her ass. She was my secretary.

jokes.text: He's not doing that. He's ordering dinner from the McDonald's on Alameda. / [Sparse laughter] [Drum roll, fanfare] [More laughter]
jokes.setup: Hank explains stage manager's headset is for technical communication
jokes.punchline: Claims he's actually ordering McDonald's, followed by Jake's drum punctuation
jokes.explanation: Standard warm-up material improved by Jake's musical punctuation

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He's not doing that. He's ordering dinner from the McDonald's on Alameda.

jokes.text: It's, uh, Jake, actually, Hank, in case you forgot. / Jake. Jack Woodward-- we're very fortunate to have him here. We outbid some of the finest high-school marching bands to get him tonight.
jokes.setup: Hank calls Jake by wrong name, Jake corrects him
jokes.punchline: Hank continues with wrong name and insulting introduction
jokes.explanation: Good escalating hostility from Hank - petty name mistakes and professional insults

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: It's, uh, Jake, actually, Hank, in case you forgot.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Jake. Jack Woodward-- we're very fortunate to have him here. We outbid some of the finest high-school marching bands to get him tonight.

jokes.text: I do miss my baton and furry hat.
jokes.setup: Hank's insult about high school marching bands
jokes.punchline: Jake playing along with the marching band joke
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating response that disarms Hank's attack

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: I do miss my baton and furry hat.

jokes.text: Interesting when those drugs just kick in like that, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Jake makes a coherent joke
jokes.punchline: Hank's inappropriate drug reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's jealousy leading him to inappropriate comments. More cringe than funny.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Interesting when those drugs just kick in like that, isn't it?

jokes.text: Especially since those charges against having sex with minors has been dropped. It has been dropped, hasn't it? / [Silence]
jokes.setup: Hank building up Jake's credentials
jokes.punchline: Sudden shift to child molestation 'joke' met with stunned silence
jokes.explanation: Deliberately awful joke that falls flat - shows Hank's desperation and poor judgment

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Especially since those charges against having sex with minors has been dropped. It has been dropped, hasn't it?
[Silence]

jokes.text: Signs over here! That say 'applesauce.' No, they don't. They say 'applause.'
jokes.setup: Hank trying to recover from his bombing
jokes.punchline: Returning to his opening joke that worked earlier
jokes.explanation: Desperate callback to earlier success after bombing - shows Hank's panic

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Signs over here! That say 'applesauce.' No, they don't. They say 'applause.'

jokes.text: I can't believe you're bringing him dinner at his office. / It's romantic. / It's pathetic.
jokes.setup: Francine preparing to bring Larry dinner
jokes.punchline: Dora's blunt assessment of the romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Good sister dynamic - Dora as voice of harsh reality vs Francine's optimism

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: I can't believe you're bringing him dinner at his office.

dialogue.speaker: Francine
dialogue.line: It's romantic.

dialogue.speaker: Dora
dialogue.line: It's pathetic.

jokes.text: I think I confused them. I think they actually thought I meant miners. You know, down in the mines.
jokes.setup: Jake defending his earlier joke about minors/miners confusion
jokes.punchline: His earnest explanation of the wordplay misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Jake's naive attempt to salvage Hank's terrible joke shows his diplomatic nature but also his misunderstanding of the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: I think I confused them. I think they actually thought I meant miners. You know, down in the mines.

jokes.text: I think I confused them. I think they actually thought I meant miners. You know, down in the mines.
jokes.setup: Jake trying to explain his confusion about Hank's child molestation joke
jokes.punchline: Suggesting audience confused 'minors' with 'miners'
jokes.explanation: Weak attempt to rationalize Hank's bombing - homophone confusion doesn't help

dialogue.speaker: Jake
dialogue.line: I think I confused them. I think they actually thought I meant miners. You know, down in the mines.

jokes.text: I wear this girdle for medical reasons, all right? I am performing with pain. / Christ.
jokes.setup: Jake mentions Hank's girdle
jokes.punchline: Hank's defensive over-explanation of medical necessity
jokes.explanation: Good Hank vulnerability moment - dignity maintained through medical excuse

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I wear this girdle for medical reasons, all right? I am performing with pain.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Christ.

jokes.text: And you're not, dickface. You are a joke. A joke!
jokes.setup: Hank claiming he's funny while Jake isn't
jokes.punchline: The irony of calling someone else a joke while being obviously unfunny
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Hank's lack of self-awareness in heated moment

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: And you're not, dickface. You are a joke. A joke!

jokes.text: Take your tea and your crumpets and, uh, queen mum and shove them up your jolly old rectum.
jokes.setup: Hank and Jake's ongoing British-themed feud
jokes.punchline: Hank's attempt at British-themed insult with anatomical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Typical Hank - trying to be sophisticated but ending crude

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Take your tea and your crumpets and, uh, queen mum and shove them up your jolly old rectum.

jokes.setup: Physical fight over equipment
jokes.punchline: Hank asserting ownership by including his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Hank's ego showing even in physical altercation - must include his branding

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: This equipment belongs to the Larry Sanders show, featuring me, 'hey, now' Hank Kingsley!

jokes.text: Was she, uh... upset? / What do you think?
jokes.setup: Larry's oblivious question about Francine's emotional state
jokes.punchline: Artie's deadpan response to the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending beat - Larry's cluelessness met with Artie's rhetorical question
